jobs.name: Flock Safety X negative-post PR monitor
jobs.prompt:
Every 6 hours, monitor X.com for recent negative posts about Flock Safety and email Jen every run using the dedicated TrybeMedia Hermes email account. If meaningful negative posts are found, send the full risk report. If no meaningful negative posts are found, send a quick no-threat report stating that no negative threats were found.

Email destination:
- Send all monitor reports from `Hermes Reports <hermes-agent@trybemedia.com>` to Jen at jennifercbonnett@gmail.com only using the configured Himalaya SMTP/IMAP account named `hermes`.
- Use Himalaya SMTP, not Google Workspace OAuth/Gmail API. Himalaya is installed at `/opt/data/profiles/tk/home/.local/bin/himalaya` and configured in `/opt/data/profiles/tk/home/.config/himalaya/config.toml`.
- Send email non-interactively by writing a plain-text RFC822/MML message to a temp file and piping it to Himalaya, for example:
  ```bash
  cat > /tmp/x-risk-report-email.txt <<'EMAIL'
  From: Hermes Reports <hermes-agent@trybemedia.com>
  To: jennifercbonnett@gmail.com
  Subject: Flock Safety X Risk Monitor — [date/time ET]

  [plain-text report body]
  EMAIL
  HIMALAYA_CONFIG=/opt/data/profiles/tk/home/.config/himalaya/config.toml /opt/data/profiles/tk/home/.local/bin/himalaya template send < /tmp/x-risk-report-email.txt
  ```
- Do not send Telegram messages. The cron job delivery target is local-only; the user-facing report should be the Himalaya SMTP email above.

Target company:
- Company: Flock Safety
- X.com handle: @Flock_Safety
- Treat references to "Flock Safety", "FlockSafety", "Flock cameras", "Flock license plate readers", "Flock LPR", and @Flock_Safety as potentially relevant when context clearly points to the company.

Self-contained workflow:
1. Use the official X/Twitter CLI `xurl`. In this Docker environment the working authenticated install is `/opt/data/home/.local/bin/xurl`, and it MUST be run with `HOME=/opt/data/home` so it can read the correct OAuth token store. Do not use the profile-local `xurl` on PATH (`/opt/data/profiles/tk/home/.local/bin/xurl`), because that wrapper is broken/misconfigured. First run only `HOME=/opt/data/home /opt/data/home/.local/bin/xurl --help` and `HOME=/opt/data/home /opt/data/home/.local/bin/xurl auth status`; for actual reads/searches use `HOME=/opt/data/home /opt/data/home/.local/bin/xurl ...`. Never read or print `~/.xurl`, never use verbose flags, and never pass inline credentials/secrets.
2. If `xurl` is unavailable, auth is not configured, or X API search is blocked by credits/permissions/rate limits, send a concise setup-needed/blocked email to jennifercbonnett@gmail.com only, explaining what failed. Do not invent findings.
3. Search X recent posts for English-language negative commentary about Flock Safety. Use one or more queries such as:
   - `("Flock Safety" OR @Flock_Safety OR FlockSafety OR "Flock cameras" OR "Flock LPR" OR "license plate readers") (bad OR terrible OR awful OR hate OR broken OR outage OR unsafe OR dangerous OR privacy OR surveillance OR creepy OR lawsuit OR unconstitutional OR unethical OR disappointed OR cancelled OR boycott OR scam OR police OR data OR breach) -is:retweet lang:en`
   - `("Flock Safety" OR @Flock_Safety OR FlockSafety) (criticism OR concerns OR backlash OR controversy OR complaint OR "not good" OR "don't trust" OR privacy OR surveillance OR lawsuit OR police) -is:retweet lang:en`
   - `(@Flock_Safety OR "Flock Safety") -is:retweet lang:en`
   Retrieve up to about 50 results if possible. Prefer posts from the last 6 hours. If the CLI cannot filter by time, use recent search results and timestamps if present.
   To capture author follower counts, prefer using the raw X API recent-search endpoint through xurl with expansions/user fields, for example:
   `HOME=/opt/data/home /opt/data/home/.local/bin/xurl --app 2056816846112735233jen_bonnett '/2/tweets/search/recent?query=[URL_ENCODED_QUERY]&max_results=50&tweet.fields=created_at,public_metrics,author_id&expansions=author_id&user.fields=username,name,public_metrics,verified'`
   Parse `includes.users[].public_metrics.followers_count` by matching each tweet's `author_id`. If raw endpoint access fails but the shortcut search works, fall back to shortcut search and mark follower count as unavailable rather than failing the whole monitor.

4. Filter out irrelevant posts, neutral news, spam, duplicates, obvious bot/engagement bait, and posts not actually about Flock Safety the company. Treat a post as relevant only if it expresses criticism, concern, frustration, reputational risk, accusation, customer impact, policy concern, privacy/surveillance concern, civil-liberties concern, security/data concern, legal/regulatory concern, product reliability complaint, law-enforcement/community backlash, or similar negative sentiment.
5. Maintain a local state file at `~/.cache/hermes/flock_safety_x_negative_seen_ids.txt` containing X post IDs already reported. Before reporting, skip IDs already in that file. After reporting, append newly reported IDs. Create the parent directory if needed. If the state file cannot be written, still email findings and mention that deduplication state could not be saved.
6. If no new meaningful negative posts are found, send a quick no-threat email report to jennifercbonnett@gmail.com only. Email format:
   - Subject: `Flock Safety X Risk Monitor — No Negative Threats — [date/time ET]`
   - Body title: `Flock Safety X Risk Monitor — No Negative Threats`
   - One-sentence summary: `No new meaningful negative X posts or negative threats about Flock Safety were found in this monitoring run.`
   - Include the search window/date-time checked and a note that the monitor will continue watching.
   - Keep this report brief; do not include long methodology unless there was a setup/API issue.
7. If new meaningful negative posts are found, draft and send a concise PR/customer-risk report to jennifercbonnett@gmail.com only. Email format:
   - Subject: `Flock Safety X Risk Monitor — [date/time ET]`
   - Body title: `Flock Safety X Risk Monitor — [date/time ET]`
   - Executive summary: 2-3 bullets on the overall pattern.
   - Themes: group the criticism into themes such as privacy/surveillance, policing/civil liberties, data/security, product reliability, legal/regulatory, customer/community backlash, misinformation risk, or competitive positioning.
   - Suggested PR/customer-success actions: practical next steps, response posture, monitoring recommendation, and whether outreach/escalation is warranted.
   - Notable posts: each with author/handle if available, author follower count if available, timestamp if available, link or post ID, concise paraphrase, engagement metrics (reply/like/retweet/quote counts if available), and why it matters. Use follower count together with engagement/replies to judge whether a response or escalation is warranted.
   - Keep it factual and do not overstate virality or sentiment beyond the observed posts.
8. Do not post, reply, like, follow, DM, or otherwise write to X. This monitor is read-only.
9. After attempting to send the Gmail report, include a short local cron summary stating whether the no-threat or alert email was sent, the Gmail API response id/threadId if available, and how many new posts were included. This local summary should not be delivered to Telegram.

jobs.name: Flock Safety Facebook Apify posts monitor
Flock Safety LinkedIn Apify posts monitor
Flock Safety Reddit Apify posts monitor
Flock Safety Instagram/Reels Apify pilot monitor
Flock Safety TikTok Apify posts monitor
Flock Safety cross-platform customer-facing summary draft
jobs.prompt:
You are preparing a CUSTOMER-FACING executive email draft for Flock Safety based on the latest internal social/reputation monitoring reports. A data-collection script has printed a source packet containing the latest outputs from the Flock Safety monitors: X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Reddit, Instagram/Reels, and TikTok.

Task:
1. Review the source packet carefully. Use only facts present in the source packet. Do not invent posts, authors, engagement, themes, or risk levels.
2. Prepare ONE polished customer-facing email draft that TrybeMedia can forward/share with Flock Safety.
3. Send the draft email via Himalaya SMTP to Jen and Genna only:
   - To: jennifercbonnett@gmail.com
   - Cc: gkeller@trevelinokeller.com
   - From: Hermes Reports <hermes-agent@trybemedia.com>
   - Use: HIMALAYA_CONFIG=/opt/data/profiles/tk/home/.config/himalaya/config.toml /opt/data/profiles/tk/home/.local/bin/himalaya template send
4. Also save the exact sent email body to /opt/data/social-monitors/customer-summaries/ with a timestamped .txt filename.

Customer-facing style requirements:
- Subject format: `DRAFT: Flock Safety Social Listening Executive Summary — [date/time ET]`
- Keep the body high-level and executive-ready.
- Include sections:
  A. Executive Summary
  B. Cross-Platform Themes
  C. Recommended Actions
  D. Most Notable Posts / Threads
  E. Monitoring Notes
- Do NOT include internal implementation details such as Apify actor names, cost caps, job IDs, scripts, cron output paths, raw dataset IDs, or tool errors unless there is a customer-relevant data limitation.
- Do NOT dump every post. Include only the most notable posts/threads across all platforms, ranked by customer relevance, severity, engagement, and novelty.
- If a source platform had no findings, mention it briefly only in Monitoring Notes.
- If X source output only provides a count/summary and not post-level details, use it at the theme/count level and do not invent X post details.
- Frame recommended actions constructively: communications posture, customer-success readiness, FAQ/talking points, escalation candidates, monitoring follow-up.
- Avoid alarmist language. Use careful wording like “observed,” “appears,” “early signal,” “limited public sample,” and “not necessarily representative.”

After sending, your final response should be a short local summary with: email sent yes/no, subject, saved file path, and which source platforms were included.

jobs.name: Flock Safety X general sentiment monitor
jobs.prompt: Every 6 hours, monitor X.com for recent public posts about Flock Safety for GENERAL SENTIMENT, not just negative mentions. Search for recent posts containing brand terms such as "Flock Safety", "Flock cameras", "Flock LPR", "Flock license plate reader", and @Flock_Safety. Use the existing authenticated xurl command via `HOME=/opt/data/home /opt/data/home/.local/bin/xurl` if available. Do not print or expose tokens. Classify relevant posts as positive, mixed/neutral, or negative and assign a sentiment score from 1-100 where 100 is fully positive and 1 is strongly negative. Include notable positive and negative posts, engagement where available, URLs, and a brief rationale. Deduplicate against a local state file under `/opt/data/social-monitors/state/sentiment/flock_safety_x_sentiment_seen.json` so repeat runs do not resend the same X URLs. Send an email report every run from `Hermes Reports <hermes-agent@trybemedia.com>` using Himalaya (`/opt/data/profiles/tk/home/.local/bin/himalaya`, config `/opt/data/profiles/tk/home/.config/himalaya/config.toml`) to `jennifercbonnett@gmail.com` only. Subject format: `Flock Safety X General Sentiment — Score NN/100 — YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM AM/PM ET`. Save any local audit/output files under `/opt/data/social-monitors/sentiment/x/`. Keep the report internal; do not include implementation secrets.

jobs.name: Flock Safety Facebook general sentiment report
Flock Safety LinkedIn general sentiment report
Flock Safety Reddit general sentiment report
Flock Safety Instagram/Reels general sentiment report
Flock Safety TikTok general sentiment report
Flock Safety general sentiment executive summary draft
jobs.prompt:
You are preparing a CUSTOMER-FACING executive email draft for Flock Safety based on the latest cross-platform GENERAL SENTIMENT social monitoring reports. A data-collection script has printed a source packet containing latest general-sentiment reports from X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Reddit, Instagram/Reels, and TikTok.

Task:
1. Review the source packet carefully. Use only facts present in the source packet. Do not invent posts, authors, engagement, URLs, themes, or risk levels.
2. Prepare one polished customer-facing email draft suitable for Trybe/TK to share with Flock Safety.
3. Use the Pyramid Principle writing style throughout: lead with the bottom-line answer first, then organize support in a clear hierarchy. Do not build slowly toward the conclusion.
4. The first paragraph under Executive Summary must be the top-line conclusion in 1-3 sentences: overall sentiment, what changed or matters most, and the recommended executive response.
5. After the top-line conclusion, group support into 3-5 MECE-style key points/themes. Each theme should start with the takeaway sentence first, followed by concise evidence from the source reports.
6. Include an overall sentiment score from 1-100 where 100 is fully positive and 1 is strongly negative. Explain in one concise sentence what drove the score.
7. Include platform-level sentiment scores when available from the source reports.
8. Structure the email with: Subject line, Executive Summary, Overall Sentiment Score, Key Themes, Recommended Actions, Most Notable Posts/Mentions, and Coverage Notes.
9. Within Recommended Actions, lead with the recommendation first, then the rationale.
10. Cover positive, mixed/neutral, and negative themes. Do not focus only on negative content.
11. Include only the most notable posts/mentions, prioritizing strong sentiment, meaningful engagement, and customer-relevant themes. For each notable post, state why it matters before adding supporting detail.
12. Keep implementation details out of the customer-facing email: no job IDs, actor names, dataset IDs, local file paths, cost caps, or debugging notes.
13. Mention coverage limitations in plain language only.
14. Send the draft email using Himalaya from `Hermes Reports <hermes-agent@trybemedia.com>` to `jennifercbonnett@gmail.com` and `gkeller@trevelinokeller.com`. Subject format: `DRAFT: Flock Safety General Sentiment Executive Summary — YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM AM/PM ET`.
15. Save a copy of the final email body under `/opt/data/social-monitors/customer-summaries/` with a filename starting `flock_safety_general_sentiment_exec_summary_`.

This is a new separate sentiment-summary stream. The existing negative/risk executive summary remains unchanged.
